findings.title: Sleep-based assertion makes the new concurrency-limit test timing-dependent
findings.why: This test infers that no more than two stats-file opens can start by sleeping for a fixed 100ms window after the first two opens begin. That is still wall-clock synchronization, so the result depends on scheduler timing instead of an explicit happens-before signal.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: The new coverage for `getReadRangeFromPropsConcurrency` can miss real regressions on slow or contended CI runners, or fail intermittently when goroutine scheduling does not line up with the fixed sleep window.
findings.evidence: After the test waits for two `startedCh` notifications, it calls `time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)` and then asserts `store.max.Load() == 2` at line 231. There is no explicit synchronization proving that a third `Open` could not start after that sleep or that the scheduler had already exercised the blocked path before the assertion.
findings.change_request: add UT for it with an explicit barrier instead of `time.Sleep`: keep the first two opens blocked, assert a third open cannot start before release via a channel-based signal or another deterministic condition, then release the barrier and verify the helper returns cleanly.
